description:
To properly set the owner of /var/log/gdm/*, run the command: 
$ sudo chown root /var/log/gdm/*
rationale:
The <tt>/var/log/gdm</tt> directory contains information about the GDM daemon
and should only be accessed by authorized personnel.
ocil_clause: /var/log/gdm/* does not have an owner of root
ocil:
To check the ownership of /var/log/gdm/*, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /var/log/gdm/*
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following owner: root
fixtext:
To properly set the owner of /var/log/gdm/*, run the command: 
$ sudo chown root /var/log/gdm/*
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /var/log/gdm/* file must be owned by root.
title: Verify Ownership of Files in /var/log/gdm
